intent: ADVERSARIAL parser-tolerance: the kernel could not resolve the SIDs to context strings, so the record carries numeric ssid=42 / tsid=99 instead of scontext= / tcontext= (avc.c:709,714). The parser must TOLERATE the fallback (not crash on missing scontext/tcontext) and DECLINE cleanly - with no type names there is no TE allow to suggest.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic). Here NEITHER layer can produce a result: with only numeric ssid/tsid and no context strings there are no types to map to a TE rule and nothing for audit2why to replay. The correct behavior is a clean parser-level decline (tolerate-and-skip), not a crash.

oracle.audit2allow_N: (empty - audit2allow emits zero rules because it has no scontext/tcontext types to work with)
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: N/A - decline scenario. Both -N and -R are empty (verified). expected.te is intentionally rule-free (header + a single class require, zero allow rules) and compiles to prove the declined/empty shape is valid.
oracle.audit2why_category: (empty - audit2why also emits nothing; no contexts to replay against policy)

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: Unresolvable SIDs: the record carries numeric ssid=42 / tsid=99 with no scontext/tcontext (the kernel could not resolve the SIDs to context strings). A TE allow needs the source-domain and target TYPE names, which are absent. Decline cleanly and note the record could not be fully resolved.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A parser that assumes scontext= / tcontext= are ALWAYS present (positional or regex with required keys) would CRASH or mis-parse on the ssid=/tsid= fallback. The robust behavior is to tolerate the fallback and decline, never panic. audit2allow itself simply emits nothing (it does not crash), which is the floor a correct impl must at least match.
